name: Pro-Home, INC.
description_short: HUD-certified non-profit housing counselor serving Southeastern Massachusetts with free one-on-one and group counseling on homebuying, foreclosure, and financial literacy.
description_long:
Pro-Home, Inc. is a 501(c)(3) non-profit corporation based in Taunton, Massachusetts, organized under Chapter 180 of the General Laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. The organization was established to produce and protect affordable housing, improve financial literacy, eliminate housing discrimination, and advocate for affordable housing access for low-to-moderate income and diverse populations. Pro-Home is certified by HUD, DHCD (Massachusetts Department of Housing and Community Development), and the MassHousing Partnership, ensuring counseling meets federal and state standards.

Pro-Home offers one-on-one and group housing counseling delivered in person or by phone, covering pre-purchase counseling, first-time homebuyer education, foreclosure and short sale assistance, tenant and landlord education, financial literacy programs, and fair housing guidance. The organization conducts regular First Time Homebuyer Seminars (2-night workshops offered six times per year), produces monthly educational videos on single-topic housing issues, and maintains community resource directories for residents needing services beyond their scope.

The organization is distinguished by its deep community integration, with a board of directors including representatives from local lenders (BankFive, Bluestone Bank, Bristol County Savings Bank), real estate associations, credit unions, and community partners. Pro-Home also manages rental properties with low-to-moderate income tenants and assists city, town, and state organizations including the Massachusetts Attorney General's Office with property renovation. The organization explicitly provides ADA accessibility and interpreters for deaf, hard of hearing, and non-English speaking residents.

Pro-Home represents legitimate, federally-certified housing counseling appropriate for individuals seeking non-commercial guidance on homeownership and financial management. The primary limitation is geographic scope—services are primarily designed for Southeastern Massachusetts residents, though the website states they are "open to assist all in need." Prospective clients should verify current availability and whether phone/remote counseling serves their location.
pros: HUD-certified, DHCD-certified, and MassHousing Partnership-certified organization ensuring compliance with federal and state standards
Offers free one-on-one and group counseling with no profit motive or sales pressure
Multiple service modalities: in-person and phone counseling to accommodate different needs
Regular First Time Homebuyer Seminars offered 6 times per year (24 hours of instruction total)
Monthly educational video series addressing specific housing and financial topics
Strong community partnerships with local lenders, realtors, credit unions, and legal professionals
ADA accessibility and interpreter services for deaf, hard of hearing, and non-English speaking residents
Manages rental properties directly, demonstrating practical expertise in low-income housing
cons: Primary service area is Southeastern Massachusetts; out-of-region residents may have limited access despite stated openness to assist all
Financial Literacy Seminar listed as "Coming Soon" with no scheduled dates, suggesting limited availability of that specific offering
Website lacks information on staff credentials, specific counselor certifications, or detailed descriptions of counseling methodologies
No information on wait times, appointment availability, or how quickly counseling can be accessed
Limited online resources; most detailed guidance requires direct contact or in-person attendance at classes
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Southeastern Massachusetts seeking pre-purchase education and guidance
Homeowners facing foreclosure or short sale situations needing HUD-certified counseling
Renters and landlords needing education on tenant rights, responsibilities, and fair housing laws
Low-to-moderate income individuals seeking free financial literacy and homeownership planning
diagnosis: Pro-Home, Inc. is best for Southeastern Massachusetts residents (particularly first-time homebuyers and those facing foreclosure) seeking legitimate, free, HUD-certified housing counseling with no commercial agenda. The primary caveat is geographic focus—while theoretically open to all, the organization is designed primarily for and markets to residents of Southeastern Massachusetts, so out-of-region callers should confirm service availability before relying on them.
services: One-on-one housing counseling (in-person and by phone)
Monthly educational video series on housing topics
Interpreter services for deaf, hard of hearing, and non-English speaking residents
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: HUD-certified nonprofit providing free housing counseling on homebuying, foreclosure, and financial literacy in Southeastern Massachusetts.
